now: Pick one hero offer and one channel stack for today.
Frame the day inside the revenue-now lane first, not across the full catalog.
Use one KPI target tonight: hero units, sticker attach rate, or bundle orders.
next: Press subscription upsells harder as the retention / recurring lane.
Turn Speedfest, Sick Summer 2026, and Syclone into premium board-level campaigns instead of general merch noise.
Build a short evergreen hero list so winning SKUs stay visible and reusable.
watch: Protect / Low Stock is 0, so good sellers can burn out fast if promoted blindly.
Out of Stock Winners is 0, which means restock gaps are distorting what looks sellable.
Tickets, subscriptions, stickers, hats, keychains, and giveaway merch still deserve heavier attention than random long-tail items.
problems: Discount and code attribution is still weak from the current export.
Catalog noise is too high, which makes decision weight too spread out.
Evergreen merch, event merch, and promo / lead-gen offers need cleaner separation.
moves: Homepage: feature the clearest hero SKU.
Email: lead with one hook, not a mixed catalog blast.
IG story: mirror the same offer stack as the homepage.
Cleanup lane: separate restock winners, protected low-stock items, and true push candidates.

homepage.title: Feature the clearest hero SKU
homepage.headline: Today needs one clear hero offer.
homepage.subhead: Pair the hero with one easy add-on and one clear reason to buy today.

email.subject: Today's Sick feature is live
email.preview: One clear hero offer, not a mixed catalog push.
email.angle: Lead with one hero offer and keep the rest of the catalog quiet.
email.body: Today's move is simple: one hero product, one add-on, one reason to buy now.

igStory: Frame 1: one clean hero product for today.
Frame 2: Add one easy low-friction extra.
Frame 3: Show the offer stack clearly.

kpi.title: Tonight's read
kpi.target: Track hero units, sticker attach rate, and bundle orders.
kpi.rule: Pick one number that decides whether the push stays tomorrow.
